settings.label: Aliangé Education
settings.headline: What Is a Peptide in Skincare?
settings.subheadline: Peptides are short chains of amino acids that function as signalling molecules within the body. In skin biology, peptides play an important role in cellular communication — helping skin cells signal when to produce structural proteins such as collagen and elastin, repair damaged tissue, and maintain the integrity of the skin barrier.

settings.text: Understand how peptides support skin structure and repair.
Learn about signal, carrier, and neurotransmitter peptides.
Discover the Aliangé Jellyfish Peptide technology.

settings.label: The Science
settings.headline: How Peptides Support Skin Structure
settings.body:
The skin relies on several key structural proteins to maintain firmness, elasticity, and resilience. These include collagen (structural support), elastin (stretch and return), and hyaluronic acid (hydration within tissue).

Over time, natural production of these molecules slows due to aging and environmental stress such as ultraviolet radiation. Peptides can act as biological messengers that encourage skin cells to continue producing these essential components.

Rather than acting as surface-level cosmetic ingredients, peptides support the biological processes that maintain healthy skin structure.

settings.label: Types of Peptides
settings.headline: Understanding Peptide Categories
settings.body:
Signal Peptides communicate with fibroblast cells, encouraging them to support collagen production. These peptides help maintain skin firmness and resilience over time.

Carrier Peptides help transport trace minerals that support enzymatic processes within the skin.

Neurotransmitter Peptides are designed to influence the interaction between nerves and muscles in the skin, helping soften the appearance of expression lines.

settings.label: The Technology
settings.headline: Jellyfish Peptide Technology
settings.body:
Aliangé's Jellyfish Peptide X Antioxidant Serum uses advanced peptide technology designed to support skin structure and protect against environmental stress.

Acetyl Hexapeptide-51 Amide — This peptide is studied for its ability to support DNA protection within skin cells. It helps strengthen the skin's natural defence mechanisms against environmental damage.

Palmitoyl Tripeptide-38 — A peptide associated with supporting the skin's extracellular matrix, which contributes to the structural network that maintains skin firmness and elasticity.

Together these peptides help support collagen production, skin elasticity, structural resilience, and reduction in visible fine lines.

settings.question: What do peptides do in skincare?
settings.answer: Peptides are short chains of amino acids that function as signalling molecules. They help skin cells communicate to produce structural proteins like collagen and elastin, supporting skin firmness, elasticity, and repair.

settings.question: What makes the Jellyfish Peptide Serum different?
settings.answer: The serum combines Acetyl Hexapeptide-51 Amide for DNA protection with Palmitoyl Tripeptide-38 for extracellular matrix support. This dual approach addresses both defence and structural renewal.

settings.question: What is Kakadu Plum?
settings.answer: Kakadu Plum is a native Australian fruit containing up to 100 times more vitamin C than oranges. It provides potent antioxidant protection and supports collagen formation.

settings.question: Can I layer this with other products?
settings.answer: Yes. The Jellyfish Peptide X Antioxidant Serum is designed for daily use and layers seamlessly within the broader Aliangé protocol, complementing barrier support and sun protection steps.

settings.question: How long until I see results?
settings.answer: You may notice improvements in hydration and radiance within weeks. Structural benefits such as improved firmness and reduced fine lines typically develop over 8–12 weeks of consistent use.
